what_it_does: Defines a small HTML preview card for an icon library, describing 99 SVG icons and rendering a selected grid of icon glyphs from a JavaScript icon registry.
observations: Contains a @dsCard metadata comment with group, name, subtitle, and viewport.
Loads shared card styling from _card.css and icon data from ../assets/icons/cv-icons.js.
Uses local CSS for card layout, lead text, a 12-column icon grid, glyph containers, and metadata text.
Lead copy states the icons live in assets/icons/svg/, use single-stroke 1.5 px artwork on a 24 x 24 grid, and can be rendered with CvIcon.
Includes a link to ../assets/icons/index.html labeled as an explorer.
Defines a SHOW array of icon names and renders any matching window.CV_ICONS.data entries into inline SVG elements.
Lists icon categories in a compact metadata line.
standouts: Client-side rendering pulls SVG path bodies from window.CV_ICONS.data by name.
The preview shows a curated subset of icons while the copy describes the full 99-icon library.
The file combines design-system documentation text with an executable visual sample.
conventions: Uses inline page-local CSS scoped by class names.
Relies on CSS custom properties for typography and colors.
Uses semantic HTML lightly, with a card div, paragraph, grid div, and metadata div.
Builds repeated SVG markup with template literals and innerHTML concatenation.
concerns: innerHTML concatenation is used for repeated SVG insertion.
The script assumes window.CV_ICONS.data exists after loading cv-icons.js.
Missing icons are ignored without visible fallback or logging.
Inline code styling is repeated directly on code elements.
notes: This file is a design-system preview card that documents and displays a sample of the icon library.
questions: How many of the 99 icons are intended to appear in this overview grid?
Should missing icon names be surfaced in the preview?
Is the @dsCard comment consumed by a preview/indexing tool?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it presents an icon library overview card and renders sample icons from the referenced icon data.
intention_signals: It appears intended to brief viewers on the icon library's size, visual rules, usage path, and available categories while showing representative glyphs.
novelty: The file uses a metadata comment for card indexing and dynamically assembles a static-looking icon overview from a global icon registry.
contribution: Contributes a compact icon-library overview pattern: metadata-tagged preview card, explanatory usage copy, category summary, and dynamic rendering of representative SVG glyphs from named registry entries.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML design-system preview card that documents a 99-icon SVG library and renders selected icons from window.CV_ICONS.data into a 12-column grid.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting the icon library as a compact branded overview with usage guidance, category context, and a visual sample set.
